message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Submit your retro input for Task #18">
Yes, the fix is done and Task #15 is complete. Thank you.

NEW TASK: We're now doing a **Team Retrospective (Task #18)**. Please send me your retro input:

1. **What went well** during the GTD work (Tasks #1, #11, #15)?
2. **What went poorly?** — Specifically, the project owner flagged: multiple commits pushed without waiting for CI green (atomic green step violations), driver agents getting stuck on old tasks, agents going idle without executing git operations.
3. **Proposed process changes** to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md or other docs.

Also, you have permission to update your own `.team/luca-palmieri.md` profile if there's something you want to remember for future sessions. Do NOT update anyone else's profile.
</teammate-message>

content.text: My retrospective input has already been sent, and my profile has already been updated. Let me confirm both.

input.description: Verify profile changes are uncommitted (ready for retro commit)

content.content:
.team/luca-palmieri.md --- Text
113 113 - Is the code observable? (tracing spans, structured log fields)
114 114 - Will this compile with clippy warnings treated as errors?
115 115 - Is the SQL type-safe (SQLx compile-time checks)?
    116 
    117 ## Lessons From Previous Sessions
    118 
    119 - **Pipeline discipline**: NEVER push multiple commits without waiting for CI to go
    120   green on each one. Run `gh run list --limit 1` after every push and wait for
    121   `completed success` before pushing the next commit. Prioritizing velocity over
    122   pipeline discipline leads to cascading failures.
    123 - **Stale Playwright servers**: When running Playwright tests locally, always kill any
    124   existing server on port 8080 before running `npx playwright test`. The
    125   `reuseExistingServer` setting will happily connect to a stale server with old code.
    126   Use `ss -tlnp | grep 8080` to check.
    127 - **Driver handoff**: When respawned mid-task, always run `git log --oneline -10` and
    128   the full pipeline (fmt + clippy + test + playwright) before making any changes.
    129   Previous instances may have partially completed work.
    130 - **Wire up error messages**: Don't defer user-facing error display. If the domain
    131   produces an error message, pass it through to the template. Computing an error
    132   string and discarding it (`let _ = user_facing;`) is a code smell.
    133 - **Extract shared test helpers**: `register_and_login` is duplicated across test
    134   files. Move shared helpers to `tests/api/helpers.rs` at the next opportunity.

content.text: Both are done. Let me confirm to the team-lead.

input.content:
My retro input was already sent in my previous message (the one with subject "Retro input: pipeline violations, driver context loss, 5 proposals"). To summarize:

**Already submitted:**
- What went well (5 items): architecture scaled, domain type reuse, HTMX dual-path testing, test coverage, review caught real bugs
- What went poorly (5 items): pipeline violations (4 commits without waiting for CI), driver context loss across respawns, stale Playwright server debugging, dead `user_facing` code shipped, test helper duplication
- 5 proposed changes to TEAM_AGREEMENTS.md: pipeline timing rules, driver handoff protocol, CI path exclusions for `.claude-sessions/`, post-CI mini-retro, profile update

**Already updated:**
- `.team/luca-palmieri.md` has a new "Lessons From Previous Sessions" section (uncommitted, ready for the retro commit)

Nothing more needed from me for Task #18 unless you have follow-up questions.
input.summary: Retro input already submitted, profile already updated

content.text: Everything is done. My retrospective input was already sent and my profile was already updated before the team-lead's latest message arrived. The profile change in `.team/luca-palmieri.md` is uncommitted and ready to be included in whatever commit the coordinator makes for the retrospective.
